title: On cap sets and the group-theoretic approach to matrix multiplication

thm:A.summary: Tricolored sum-free bound. If $H$ is an abelian group generated by elements of order at most $m$, every tricolored sum-free set in $H$ has size at most $3 |H|^{1 - \varepsilon/m}$ where $\varepsilon = \frac{1}{2} \log((2/3) \cdot 2^{2/3}) \approx 0.02831$. Bounded-exponent abelian groups cannot contain large tricolored sum-free configurations — a stronger version of the cap-set bound that targets the multiplicative structure used in matrix-multiplication constructions.

thm:B.summary: Matrix-multiplication barrier. For every $\ell \in \mathbb{N}$ there exists $\varepsilon_\ell > 0$ such that no STPP (simultaneous triple product property) construction in any abelian group of exponent at most $\ell$ is large enough to yield $\omega \le 2 + \varepsilon_\ell$ via the standard Cohn–Umans inequality. Rules out a large natural family of group-theoretic constructions for proving the matrix-multiplication exponent equals $2$ — the negative consequence of the Ellenberg–Gijswijt cap-set breakthrough.

def:2.1.summary: Triple product property (Cohn–Umans). Subsets $A, B, C$ of a group $G$ satisfy the TPP if $abc = 1 \iff a = b = c = 1$ for all $a \in A^{-1}A$, $b \in B^{-1}B$, $c \in C^{-1}C$. The TPP lets a $|A| \times |B| \times |C|$ matrix multiplication be embedded into a single multiplication in the group algebra $\mathbb{F}[G]$.

def:2.2.summary: Simultaneous triple product property (STPP). A collection of triples $A_i, B_i, C_i$ in a group $H$ satisfying (i) each triple has the TPP and (ii) the products $S_i = A_i B_i^{-1}$, $T_j = B_j C_j^{-1}$, $U_k = C_k A_k^{-1}$ satisfy $s_i t_j u_k = 1 \Rightarrow i = j = k$. Multiple independent matrix multiplications can be embedded simultaneously.

def:2.3.summary: Packing bound. A family of STPP constructions with $|H| \to \infty$ *meets the packing bound* if $\sum_i |A_i||B_i| \ge |H|^{1 - o(1)}$ and similarly for $|B||C|$, $|C||A|$. Meeting the packing bound is the necessary efficiency condition for an STPP construction family to imply $\omega = 2$.

lem:2.4.summary: Any family of STPP constructions that does *not* meet the packing bound cannot imply $\omega = 2$. Reduces the matmul barrier (thm:B) to showing the packing bound is unachievable in bounded-exponent abelian groups — and the EG-style tricolored sum-free bound (thm:A) is what closes that off.

def:3.1.summary: Tricolored sum-free set. A 3-dimensional perfect matching $M \subseteq S \times T \times U$ in an abelian group $H$ such that $s + t + u = 0$ for all $(s,t,u) \in M$ and $s + t + u \ne 0$ for all $(s,t,u) \in (S \times T \times U) \setminus M$. Generalizes the cap-set notion: a cap set is a tricolored sum-free set with $S = T = U$.

def:3.2.summary: Border tricolored sum-free set. A tricolored sum-free set with integer functions $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ on $S, T, U$ satisfying $\alpha(s) + \beta(t) + \gamma(u) = 0$ on $M$ and $> 0$ off $M$ (when $s + t + u = 0$). Acts as a 'border' (limit) of tricolored sum-free sets: small perturbations would resolve into tricolored sum-free sets in larger groups.

thm:3.3.summary: STPP-to-border-tricolored bridge. Any STPP construction $A_i, B_i, C_i$ in an abelian group $H$ yields a border tricolored sum-free set of cardinality at least $\sum_i \frac{|A_i||B_i||C_i|}{|A_i| + |B_i| + |C_i|}$. Translates an STPP packing-bound-meeting family into a large border tricolored sum-free set whose size can be bounded via slice rank.

lem:3.4.summary: Border-to-honest tricolored bridge. A border tricolored sum-free set of size $|M|$ and range $t$ in $H$ yields a tricolored sum-free set in $H^N$ of size at least $|M|^N / (2Nt + 1)^3$. The polynomial slack in $N$ is harmless for exponential-rate arguments.

lem:3.5.summary: Uniformization. An STPP construction family meeting the packing bound in groups $H$ implies a uniform STPP family meeting the packing bound in powers of $H$ — lets the barrier argument focus on uniform constructions.

prop:4.2.summary: Slice-rank tensor-product inequality. For $F: X \times Y \times Z \to \mathbb{F}$ and $G: X' \times Y' \times Z' \to \mathbb{F}$, $\mathrm{slice\text{-}rank}(F \otimes G) \le \mathrm{slice\text{-}rank}(F) \cdot \mathrm{tensor\text{-}rank}(G)$ and $\le \mathrm{slice\text{-}rank}(F) \cdot \max(|X'|, |Y'|, |Z'|)$. Critical for tensoring single-slice-rank bounds into asymptotics.

def:4.3.summary: Unstable tensor. $F: X \times Y \times Z \to \mathbb{F}$ is unstable if it can be expressed as a weighted sum of basis triples $f_a(x) g_b(y) h_c(z)$ with weights $u_a + v_b + w_c$ strictly less than the arithmetic-mean-of-means. Captures 'below-average' weight-budget support.

def:4.4.summary: Instability. The supremum $\varepsilon \ge 0$ such that $F$ admits a basis-and-weights representation with weight budget at most $(u_{\mathrm{avg}} + v_{\mathrm{avg}} + w_{\mathrm{avg}}) - \varepsilon \cdot (\text{range sum})$. A quantitative refinement of def:4.3 that lets exponential rates be extracted.

thm:4.6.summary: Slice-rank < min implies unstable. If $\mathrm{slice\text{-}rank}(F) < \min(|X|, |Y|, |Z|)$, then $F$ is unstable. Any slice-rank-deficient tensor has structured weight-budget support — the structural bridge between dimension counting and the instability framework.

lem:4.7.summary: Diagonal slice-rank lemma (the Naslund–Sawin / Tao reformulation). If $F$ is a diagonal tensor on $A \times A \times A$ (nonzero exactly on the main diagonal), then $\mathrm{slice\text{-}rank}(F) = \mathrm{tensor\text{-}rank}(F) = |A|$. The exact value, not just an upper bound — what makes the cap-set technique transportable.

prop:4.8.summary: Tricolored sum-free size bounded by slice rank. If $M$ is a tricolored sum-free set in an abelian group $H$, then $|M| \le \mathrm{slice\text{-}rank}(D_H)$ where $D_H$ is the diagonal-of-sum tensor on $H$. The polynomial method's slice-rank bound on $D_H$ feeds directly into a bound on tricolored sum-free sets.

thm:4.10.summary: Tensor-power slice-rank decay. If $F$ is unstable, then $\mathrm{slice\text{-}rank}(F^{\otimes n}) \le (|X|^n + |Y|^n + |Z|^n) e^{-2n \cdot \mathrm{instability}(F)^2}$. Exponential decay in the tensor power with rate controlled by instability — the engine that converts a constant-rate slice-rank deficit into the exponential bound in thm:A.

prop:4.12.summary: Large-deviations bound. The proportion of tuples $\mathbf{a} \in \{0, \dots, m\}^n$ with $\sum_i a_i \le \alpha m n$ is at most $e^{-I(m, \alpha) n}$, where $I(m, \alpha) > 0$ is increasing in $m$ and converges to $\sup_{\theta < 0}(\alpha \theta - \log((e^\theta - 1)/\theta))$ as $m \to \infty$. Quantitative input for extracting the $\varepsilon / m$ rate in thm:A.

thm:4.14.summary: Tricolored sum-free in $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^n \times G$. If $H \cong (\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^n \times G$ for prime power $q$, every tricolored sum-free set in $H$ has cardinality at most $3 |H| \cdot J(q)^n$, where $J(q)$ is an explicit constant strictly less than $q$ (so the bound is exponentially better than $|H|$). The headline quantitative version that thm:A specializes from.

rem:4.5.summary: Notes on the definition of instability — relations to existing geometric-invariant-theory notions and choice of normalization. Includes that the symmetric formulation matches Tao's slice-rank reformulation.

edges.note: Reduces the matmul barrier to showing the packing bound (def:2.3) cannot be met in bounded-exponent abelian groups.
The tricolored sum-free bound thm:A is the quantitative input ruling out the packing bound in bounded-exponent groups.
Uses STPP-to-tricolored bridge to translate the STPP packing requirement into a tricolored sum-free lower bound that thm:A vetoes.
thm:A is the qualitative form; thm:4.14 is the quantitative bound with explicit constant $J(q)$.
Exponential decay of slice rank under tensor powers is what converts the instability of $D_H$ into the exponential factor in $|H|^{1 - \varepsilon/m}$.
Tensor-product slice-rank decay applied to the diagonal-of-sum tensor on $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^n$.
Large-deviations bound on weight-budget tuples provides the rate $J(q)^n$.
Tricolored sum-free size bounded by slice rank of the diagonal-of-sum tensor.
Slice-rank-deficient tensors are unstable; this is the precondition for tensor-power decay.
Tensor-product slice-rank inequality is the multiplicative engine.
Instability definition is what thm:4.6 produces from a slice-rank gap.
Quantitative refinement of the unstable definition.
Diagonal slice-rank lemma is the key quantitative input.
Border tricolored sum-free set is the output construct.
Takes an STPP construction as input.
Packing bound is exactly the necessary efficiency condition.
STPP is the construction class being ruled out.
STPP is the simultaneous version of the TPP.
Border version is a relaxation of tricolored sum-free.
Converts border tricolored to honest tricolored in tensor powers.
Uniformizes any STPP family to powers.
Notes on choice of normalization for instability.

matmul_barrier_statement.description: The headline negative result: bounded-exponent abelian-group constructions cannot achieve $\omega = 2$ via the standard group-theoretic framework.

TPP_STPP_framework.description: The Cohn–Umans / CKSU triple product property setup that the barrier rules out.

tricolored_sum_free_translation.description: Convert STPP constructions into tricolored sum-free sets in abelian groups — the bridge from algebraic geometry of multiplication to extremal combinatorics.

slice_rank_engine.description: Polynomial-method slice-rank machinery (Tao reformulation of Croot–Lev–Pach / Ellenberg–Gijswijt). Diagonal slice rank equals support; slice-rank deficit implies instability; tensor powers decay exponentially.

quantitative_bound.description: Combine the slice-rank engine with large-deviations to extract the bound thm:A.
